title: Contributing to toolero
text: Function names follow strict verb convention based function primarily : init_() — one-time scaffolding workspace environment. Runs , creates persistent structure, gets way. Example: init_project(). create_() — produces specific file artifact user author edit directly. May called multiple times project. Example: create_qmd(). generate_() — derives assembles output inputs already exist. transformation happening. Example: generate_dockerfile(), generate_kb_xml(). read_() — loads data R session. Nothing written disk. Example: read_clean_csv(). write_() — persists data results session disk. complement read_(). Example: write_by_group().

title: Contributing to toolero
text: R source files use dashes: create-qmd.R, write--group.R R functions objects use underscores: create_qmd(), write_by_group()

title: Contributing to toolero
text: Internal helpers exported follow .function_name() convention leading dot. Example: .validate_file_arg().

title: Getting started with toolero
text: toolero grew recurring observation made teaching supporting researchers UW-Madison: habits make project reproducible, shareable, maintainable easiest adopt beginning — hardest retrofit project already underway. package heavily influenced workflows taught workshops run Carpentries UW-Madison Libraries. workshops emphasize consistent project organization, version control, reproducible data practices foundational skills — advanced topics. toolero tries operationalize principles small set functions reduce friction right thing start. theming branding support toolero specifically tailored UW-Madison’s Research Computing Instrumentation (RCI) unit, whose Quarto-based reporting templates baked package defaults. UW-Madison, branding files optional — rest package works independently .

title: Getting started with toolero
text: toolero designed researchers analysts : Work primarily R use RStudio IDE Write reports analyses Quarto Want consistent, reproducible project structure without think every time May need publish content UW-Madison Knowledge Base package intentionally small. try comprehensive. tries make right defaults easy reach first line code.

title: Getting started with toolero
text: can install toolero CRAN: install development version GitHub:
code: install.packages("toolero") pak::pak("erwinlares/toolero")

title: Getting started with toolero
text: two functions designed used together, order. init_project() creates scaffold; create_qmd() populates working Quarto document.

title: Getting started with toolero
text: Starting new R project usually means manual steps every time: create folder, set RStudio project, create subdirectories data scripts, initialize renv, initialize git. None steps hard , skipping — especially early — tends create friction later. init_project() handles single call: creates new RStudio project specified path following folder structure already place: structure? folder layout opinionated arbitrary. Separating data/ data-raw/ makes clear files original processed. Keeping R/ distinct scripts/ encourages moving reusable logic functions time, natural step toward maintainable code. default, init_project() also initializes renv git. means project reproducible version-controlled first commit. renv git default? renv ensures packages project depends recorded reproducible. git provides full history changes. much easier set start retrofit later. project needs folders beyond defaults: apply UW-Madison RCI branding assets project: creates assets/ folder populates styles.css, header.html, rci-banner.png — assets used Quarto template scaffolded create_qmd().
code: library(toolero)  init_project(path = "~/Documents/my-project") my-project/ ├── data/         # input data ├── data-raw/     # original, unprocessed data ├── R/            # reusable functions ├── scripts/      # analysis scripts ├── plots/        # generated visualizations ├── images/       # static images and assets ├── results/      # processed outputs and tables └── docs/         # notes, manuscripts, Quarto documents init_project(   path          = "~/Documents/my-project",   extra_folders = c("notebooks", "presentations") ) init_project(   path        = "~/Documents/my-project",   uw_branding = TRUE )

what: Adding a Quarto document with create_qmd()
title: Getting started with toolero
text: project exists, create_qmd() adds working Quarto document : creates: analysis.qmd — Quarto document fully populated YAML header, three-context input resolution via detect_execution_context(), sample analysis using Palmer Penguins dataset data/sample.csv — sample data develop immediately assets/styles.css assets/header.html — UW-Madison RCI branding _quarto.yml — project file post-render hook runs purl.R purl.R — extracts R code rendered document companion .R file automatically every render purl hook? plain .R companion .qmd useful sharing analysis script, running remote cluster, archiving code independently document. hook runs automatically never remember extract manually. pre-populate YAML header metadata: -metadata.yml might look like: keys present YAML file overwrite corresponding placeholders template. Keys present left -.
code: create_qmd(path = "~/Documents/my-project", filename = "analysis.qmd") create_qmd(   path      = "~/Documents/my-project",   filename  = "analysis.qmd",   yaml_data = "~/my-metadata.yml" ) title: "My Analysis" author:   - name: "Your Name"     affiliation: "UW-Madison"     email: "you@wisc.edu"

what: Working with data: read_clean_csv() and write_by_group()
title: Getting started with toolero
text: two functions address common friction points day--day data work. general-purpose utilities — useful R project, just ones set toolero.

previous_headings: Working with data: read_clean_csv() and write_by_group()
title: Getting started with toolero
text: read_clean_csv() combines readr::read_csv() janitor::clean_names() single call: Column names automatically converted lowercase underscores — consistent, predictable, tidyverse-friendly. column called First Name becomes first_name. Q1 Revenue ($) becomes q1_revenue. default, column type messages readr suppressed. Set verbose = TRUE see :
code: data <- read_clean_csv("data/my-file.csv") data <- read_clean_csv("data/my-file.csv", verbose = TRUE)

previous_headings: Working with data: read_clean_csv() and write_by_group()
what: Splitting data by group with write_by_group()
title: Getting started with toolero
text: data frame contains multiple groups need written separate files, write_by_group() handles split write single call: Output filenames derived group values sanitized use file names — converted lowercase spaces special characters replaced dashes. group called Chinstrap becomes chinstrap.csv. Palmer Penguins become palmer-penguins.csv. also write manifest listing output files, group values, row counts:
code: write_by_group(   data       = penguins,   group_col  = "species",   output_dir = "results/by-species" ) write_by_group(   data       = penguins,   group_col  = "species",   output_dir = "results/by-species",   manifest   = TRUE )

title: Getting started with toolero
text: R code often needs behave differently depending running — interactively RStudio, quarto render, batch Rscript job remote cluster. detect_execution_context() identifies three environments active returns one "interactive", "quarto", "rscript". canonical use case resolving input file paths portably: pattern built template scaffolded create_qmd(), get free without write .
code: context <- detect_execution_context()  input_file <- switch(context,   interactive = "data/sample.csv",   quarto      = params$input_file,   rscript     = commandArgs(trailingOnly = TRUE)[1] )

title: Getting started with toolero
text: section relevant publish content UW-Madison Knowledge Base. , can safely skip . UW-Madison Knowledge Base requires content submitted XML visual assets embedded HTML body. generate_kb_xml() automates process entirely. function: Infers source .qmd HTML path (accepts explicitly via qmd_path) Re-renders document embed-resources: true CSS, images, JavaScript self-contained Extracts metadata .qmd YAML header — title → kb_title, description → kb_summary, categories → kb_keywords Produces .xml file ready direct KB import description categories fields create_qmd() template matter — flow automatically KB article metadata without extra work. importing KB, check Decode HTML entity body content option.
code: generate_kb_xml(   html_path  = "docs/analysis.html",   output_dir = "exports" )

title: A Toolkit for Research Workflows
text: toolero R package designed help researchers implement best practices coding projects. provides small set opinionated, practical functions reduce friction start project day--day data work.

title: A Toolkit for Research Workflows
text: can install toolero CRAN: install development version GitHub:
code: install.packages("toolero") # install.packages("pak") pak::pak("erwinlares/toolero")

title: A Toolkit for Research Workflows
text: Creates new R project standard folder structure suited research workflows. Optionally initializes renv package management git version control. default folder structure includes: data/, data-raw/, R/, scripts/, plots/, images/, results/, docs/.
code: library(toolero)  # Create a project with the standard folder structure init_project(path = "~/Documents/my-project")  # Add extra folders init_project(path = "~/Documents/my-project",              extra_folders = c("notebooks", "presentations"))  # Skip renv and git init_project(path = "~/Documents/my-project", use_renv = FALSE, use_git = FALSE)

title: A Toolkit for Research Workflows
text: Scaffolds new Quarto document reproducible template, including sample dataset, UW-Madison branded assets, optional post-render purl hook extracts R code rendered document companion .R file. Optionally pre-populates YAML header user-supplied YAML config file.
code: library(toolero)  # Create a document with placeholder YAML create_qmd(path = "~/Documents/my-project", filename = "analysis.qmd")  # Create without the purl hook create_qmd(path = "~/Documents/my-project", filename = "report.qmd",            use_purl = FALSE)  # Pre-populate YAML from a personal config file create_qmd(path = "~/Documents/my-project", filename = "analysis.qmd",            yaml_data = "~/my_config.yml")

title: A Toolkit for Research Workflows
text: Reads CSV file cleans column names one step, producing tidyverse-friendly tibble.
code: library(toolero)  data <- read_clean_csv("path/to/file.csv")  # Show column type messages data <- read_clean_csv("path/to/file.csv", verbose = TRUE)

title: A Toolkit for Research Workflows
text: Identifies three execution environments code currently running : interactive R session, quarto render call, plain Rscript invocation. Returns one "interactive", "quarto", "rscript".
code: library(toolero)  context <- detect_execution_context()  input_file <- switch(context,   interactive = "data/sample.csv",   quarto      = params$input_file,   rscript     = commandArgs(trailingOnly = TRUE)[1] )

title: A Toolkit for Research Workflows
text: Splits data frame single grouping column writes group separate CSV file. Filenames derived sanitized group values — converted lowercase spaces special characters replaced dashes. Optionally writes manifest.csv listing output files, group values, row counts.
code: library(toolero)  # Load the bundled sample dataset sample_path <- system.file("templates", "sample.csv", package = "toolero") penguins    <- read_clean_csv(sample_path)  # Split by species write_by_group(penguins, group_col = "species", output_dir = tempdir())  # Also write a manifest write_by_group(penguins, group_col = "species",                output_dir = tempdir(), manifest = TRUE)

title: A Toolkit for Research Workflows
text: Produces UW-Madison Knowledge Base importable XML file rendered Quarto document. Re-renders source .qmd assets embedded, extracts HTML body, wraps KB XML structure along metadata drawn document’s YAML header — title → kb_title, description → kb_summary, categories → kb_keywords. importing resulting XML KB, check Decode HTML entity body content option.
code: library(toolero)  generate_kb_xml(   html_path  = "docs/analysis.html",   output_dir = "exports" )

text: cite toolero publications:
code: citation("toolero")

what: Create a new Quarto document from a template — create_qmd
title: Create a new Quarto document from a template — create_qmd
text: Creates new Quarto document specified directory, along sample dataset UW-Madison branded assets. Optionally pre-populates YAML header user-supplied metadata.

title: Create a new Quarto document from a template — create_qmd
code: create_qmd(   path = ".",   filename = NULL,   yaml_data = NULL,   overwrite = FALSE,   use_purl = TRUE )

title: Create a new Quarto document from a template — create_qmd
text: path string. Path directory document created. Defaults "." (current working directory). filename string NULL. Name generated .qmd file. Must supplied explicitly, e.g. "analysis.qmd". yaml_data string NULL. Path YAML file containing metadata pre-populate document header. NULL (default), template copied -placeholder prompts intact. overwrite logical. Whether overwrite existing files. Defaults FALSE. use_purl Logical. TRUE (default), creates _quarto.yml file post-render hook purl.R script inside R/ extracts R code rendered document .R file. target document resolved dynamically scanning project root .qmd files, purl.R works regardless document name.

title: Create a new Quarto document from a template — create_qmd
text: Invisibly returns path.

title: Create a new Quarto document from a template — create_qmd
text: create_qmd() performs following steps: Validates path exists. Validates filename supplied. Creates data/ folder path copies sample.csv . Checks assets/styles.css assets/header.html - creates assets/ folder needed copies package. Copies template .qmd path/filename. yaml_data provided, reads YAML file substitutes values document header. use_purl = TRUE, writes _quarto.yml post-render hook pointing R/purl.R, copies purl.R package templates path/R/purl.R. Note: filename default value must always supplied explicitly. Use tempdir() temporary output testing exploration.

title: Create a new Quarto document from a template — create_qmd
code: # \donttest{ # Create a document in a temp directory create_qmd(path = tempdir(), filename = "analysis.qmd") #> ✔ Created /tmp/Rtmpdhd6Gt/data/sample.csv #> ✔ Created /tmp/Rtmpdhd6Gt/assets/styles.css #> ✔ Created /tmp/Rtmpdhd6Gt/assets/header.html #> ✔ Created /tmp/Rtmpdhd6Gt/analysis.qmd #> ✔ Created /tmp/Rtmpdhd6Gt/_quarto.yml #> ✔ Created /tmp/Rtmpdhd6Gt/R/purl.R  # Create with a custom filename, without the purl hook create_qmd(path = tempdir(), filename = "report.qmd",             overwrite = TRUE, use_purl = FALSE) #> ✔ Created /tmp/Rtmpdhd6Gt/data/sample.csv #> ✔ Created /tmp/Rtmpdhd6Gt/assets/styles.css #> ✔ Created /tmp/Rtmpdhd6Gt/assets/header.html #> ✔ Created /tmp/Rtmpdhd6Gt/report.qmd  # Create with pre-populated YAML yaml_file <- tempfile(fileext = ".yml") writeLines("author:\n  - name: 'Your Name'", yaml_file) create_qmd(path = tempdir(), filename = "analysis.qmd",             yaml_data = yaml_file, overwrite = TRUE) #> ✔ Created /tmp/Rtmpdhd6Gt/data/sample.csv #> ✔ Created /tmp/Rtmpdhd6Gt/assets/styles.css #> ✔ Created /tmp/Rtmpdhd6Gt/assets/header.html #> ✔ Created /tmp/Rtmpdhd6Gt/analysis.qmd #> ✔ Created /tmp/Rtmpdhd6Gt/_quarto.yml #> ✔ Created /tmp/Rtmpdhd6Gt/R/purl.R # }

what: Detect the current execution context — detect_execution_context
title: Detect the current execution context — detect_execution_context
text: Identifies three execution environments code currently running : interactive R session, quarto render call, plain Rscript invocation. useful writing code behaves correctly across three contexts, resolving input file paths portable way.

title: Detect the current execution context — detect_execution_context
code: detect_execution_context(interactive_fn = interactive)

title: Detect the current execution context — detect_execution_context
text: interactive_fn function. Used detect whether session interactive. Defaults base::interactive. Override tests simulate different execution environments.

title: Detect the current execution context — detect_execution_context
text: character string, one "interactive", "quarto", "rscript".

title: Detect the current execution context — detect_execution_context
text: Detection follows priority order: interactive() TRUE, returns "interactive". environment variable QUARTO_DOCUMENT_PATH set non-empty, returns "quarto". Otherwise, returns "rscript".

title: Detect the current execution context — detect_execution_context
code: # \donttest{ context <- detect_execution_context()  input_file <- switch(context,   interactive = "data/sample.csv",   quarto      = params$input_file,   rscript     = commandArgs(trailingOnly = TRUE)[1] ) # }

what: Generate a KB-importable XML file from a Quarto document — generate_kb_xml
title: Generate a KB-importable XML file from a Quarto document — generate_kb_xml
text: Takes Quarto document produces XML file directly importable UW-Madison Knowledge Base (KB) article. function re-renders .qmd embed-resources: true visual assets self-contained, extracts HTML body, wraps KB XML structure along metadata drawn document's YAML header.

title: Generate a KB-importable XML file from a Quarto document — generate_kb_xml
code: generate_kb_xml(html_path, qmd_path = NULL, output_dir = NULL)

title: Generate a KB-importable XML file from a Quarto document — generate_kb_xml
text: html_path string. Path rendered HTML file. Used infer output filename , qmd_path NULL, location source .qmd. qmd_path string NULL. Path source .qmd file. NULL (default), inferred replacing .html extension html_path .qmd. output_dir string NULL. Directory .xml file written. NULL (default), written directory html_path.

title: Generate a KB-importable XML file from a Quarto document — generate_kb_xml
text: Invisibly returns path written .xml file.

title: Generate a KB-importable XML file from a Quarto document — generate_kb_xml
text: generate_kb_xml() performs following steps: Validates html_path exists. Infers qmd_path html_path supplied, validates . Extracts title, description, categories .qmd YAML header maps kb_title, kb_summary, kb_keywords. Re-renders .qmd isolated temporary directory embed-resources: true CSS, images, JS self-contained. data/ assets/ folders copied alongside .qmd ensure render succeeds. Extracts <body> embedded HTML. Escapes HTML entities body XML compatibility, required UW-Madison KB import format. Builds XML structure kb_title, kb_keywords, kb_summary, kb_body nodes. Writes .xml file output_dir. Temporary files managed via withr::local_tempdir() automatically cleaned function exits, even error. importing resulting XML KB, check Decode HTML entity body content option.

title: Generate a KB-importable XML file from a Quarto document — generate_kb_xml
code: # \donttest{ # Infer qmd_path automatically, write XML alongside the HTML # generate_kb_xml(html_path = "docs/analysis.html")  # Supply qmd_path explicitly and write to a specific output directory # generate_kb_xml( #   html_path  = "docs/analysis.html", #   qmd_path   = "analysis.qmd", #   output_dir = "exports" # ) # }

what: Initialize a new R project with a standard folder structure — init_project
title: Initialize a new R project with a standard folder structure — init_project
text: init_project() creates new R project given path opinionated folder structure suited research workflows. optionally initializes renv package management git version control.

title: Initialize a new R project with a standard folder structure — init_project
code: init_project(   path,   use_renv = TRUE,   use_git = TRUE,   extra_folders = NULL,   open = FALSE,   uw_branding = FALSE )

title: Initialize a new R project with a standard folder structure — init_project
text: path character string path name new project (e.g., "~/Documents/-project"). use_renv Logical. TRUE, initializes renv new project. Defaults TRUE. use_git Logical. TRUE, initializes git repository new project. Defaults TRUE. extra_folders character vector additional folder names create inside project. Defaults NULL. open Logical. TRUE, opens new project RStudio creation. Defaults TRUE. uw_branding Logical. TRUE, creates assets/ folder populates UW-Madison RCI branding files (styles.css, header.html, rci-banner.png). Defaults FALSE.

title: Initialize a new R project with a standard folder structure — init_project
text: Called side effects. return value.

title: Initialize a new R project with a standard folder structure — init_project
code: if (FALSE) { # \dontrun{ init_project(path = file.path(tempdir(), "project1"),              use_renv = FALSE, use_git = FALSE)  init_project(path = file.path(tempdir(), "project2"),              uw_branding = TRUE, use_renv = FALSE, use_git = FALSE)  init_project(path = file.path(tempdir(), "project3"),              extra_folders = c("notebooks"),              use_renv = FALSE, use_git = FALSE) } # }

what: Read and clean a CSV file — read_clean_csv
title: Read and clean a CSV file — read_clean_csv
text: read_clean_csv() reads CSV file cleans column names one step. leverages readr::read_csv() reading janitor::clean_names() making column names tidyverse-friendly (lowercase, spaces, special characters). default, column type messages suppressed. Set verbose = TRUE display .

title: Read and clean a CSV file — read_clean_csv
code: read_clean_csv(file_path, verbose = FALSE)

title: Read and clean a CSV file — read_clean_csv
text: file_path character string path CSV file. verbose Logical. TRUE, displays column type messages readr::read_csv(). Defaults FALSE.

title: Read and clean a CSV file — read_clean_csv
text: tibble clean column names.

title: Read and clean a CSV file — read_clean_csv
code: # \donttest{ # Read and clean a CSV file silently sample_path <- system.file("templates", "sample.csv", package = "toolero") data <- read_clean_csv(sample_path)  # Show column type messages data <- read_clean_csv(sample_path, verbose = TRUE) #> Rows: 344 Columns: 8 #> ── Column specification ──────────────────────────────────────────────────────── #> Delimiter: "," #> chr (3): species, island, sex #> dbl (5): bill_length_mm, bill_depth_mm, flipper_length_mm, body_mass_g, year #>  #> ℹ Use `spec()` to retrieve the full column specification for this data. #> ℹ Specify the column types or set `show_col_types = FALSE` to quiet this message. # }

what: Split a data frame by a grouping column and write each group to a CSV file — write_by_group
title: Split a data frame by a grouping column and write each group to a CSV file — write_by_group
text: Splits data frame single grouping column writes group separate CSV file. Optionally writes manifest file listing output files, group values, row counts.

title: Split a data frame by a grouping column and write each group to a CSV file — write_by_group
code: write_by_group(data, group_col, output_dir = NULL, manifest = FALSE)

title: Split a data frame by a grouping column and write each group to a CSV file — write_by_group
text: data data frame tibble split save. group_col string. name column group . output_dir string NULL. Path directory output files written. Created exist. NULL, user must supply path explicitly. manifest logical. Whether write manifest.csv file output_dir listing output files, group values, row counts. Defaults FALSE.

title: Split a data frame by a grouping column and write each group to a CSV file — write_by_group
text: Invisibly returns output_dir.

title: Split a data frame by a grouping column and write each group to a CSV file — write_by_group
text: Output filenames derived group values group_col. Values sanitized use filenames: converted lowercase, spaces special characters replaced -, consecutive dashes collapsed, leading/trailing dashes stripped. manifest = TRUE, manifest.csv written output_dir containing three columns: group_value, n_rows, file_path. Note: output_dir default value. Always supply explicit path avoid writing files unexpected locations. Use tempdir() temporary output testing exploration.

title: Split a data frame by a grouping column and write each group to a CSV file — write_by_group
code: # \donttest{ # Split a small data frame by group and write to a temp directory data <- data.frame(   species = c("Adelie", "Adelie", "Gentoo"),   mass    = c(3750, 3800, 5000) ) write_by_group(data, group_col = "species", output_dir = tempdir()) #> ✔ Written "Adelie" (2 rows) to /tmp/Rtmpdhd6Gt/adelie.csv #> ✔ Written "Gentoo" (1 rows) to /tmp/Rtmpdhd6Gt/gentoo.csv  # Same but also write a manifest write_by_group(data, group_col = "species",                output_dir = tempdir(), manifest = TRUE) #> ✔ Written "Adelie" (2 rows) to /tmp/Rtmpdhd6Gt/adelie.csv #> ✔ Written "Gentoo" (1 rows) to /tmp/Rtmpdhd6Gt/gentoo.csv #> ✔ Manifest written to /tmp/Rtmpdhd6Gt/manifest.csv # }

title: toolero 0.3.0
text: create_qmd(): filename longer defaults "analysis.qmd" — must now supplied explicitly. Code relied default error. write_by_group(): sanitized output filenames now use - (dash) separator instead _ (underscore), consistent package convention file names use dashes. Existing workflows reference output paths name need update accordingly. init_project(): file_path argument renamed path consistency create_qmd() broader package API. Calls using file_path = name error; positional calls unaffected.

title: toolero 0.3.0
text: create_qmd(): path now defaults "." (current working directory). Explicit NULL longer accepted. filename remains required provides sufficient protection accidental misuse.

title: toolero 0.3.0
text: create_qmd(): _quarto.yml now copied inst/templates/ rather written hardcoded string, changes template reflected automatically. create_qmd(): purl.R now correctly placed R/ instead project root, consistent _quarto.yml calling Rscript R/purl.R. inst/templates/purl.R: replaced QUARTO_DOCUMENT_PATH environment variable approach fs::dir_ls() glob scan, works reliably regardless Quarto invokes post-render script. create_qmd(): fixed YAML boolean serialization yaml_data supplied. yaml::.yaml() converting true/false yes/, Quarto recognize. custom handler now forces true/false output.

title: toolero 0.3.0
text: Added generate_kb_xml() produce UW-Madison KB-importable XML files rendered Quarto documents. Extracts metadata .qmd YAML header re-renders embedded resources self-contained import. create_qmd(): added use_purl argument (default TRUE) scaffolds _quarto.yml post-render hook purl.R script extracting R code rendered documents.

title: toolero 0.2.0
text: CRAN release: 2026-04-24

title: toolero 0.2.0
text: create_qmd(): path now required argument default. Passing NULL omitting raises error. Use tempdir() temporary output. write_by_group(): output_dir now required argument default. Passing NULL omitting raises error. Use tempdir() temporary output. init_project(): open now defaults FALSE instead TRUE avoid disrupting current RStudio session non-interactive contexts.

title: toolero 0.2.0
text: Added detect_execution_context() identify whether code running interactive R session, quarto render call, plain Rscript invocation. Returns one "interactive", "quarto", "rscript". Added create_qmd() scaffold new Quarto document reproducible template, including sample dataset, UW-Madison branded assets, three-context input resolution via detect_execution_context(). Optionally pre-populates YAML header user-supplied YAML config file. Added write_by_group() split data frame single grouping column write group separate CSV file. Filenames derived sanitized group values. Optionally writes manifest.csv listing output files, group values, row counts.

title: toolero 0.1.1
text: Added uw_branding argument init_project(). TRUE, creates assets/ folder new project populates UW-Madison RCI branding files (styles.css, header.html, rci-banner.png).

title: toolero 0.1.0
text: Initial CRAN submission.
